The '''HAVw A6 Juggernaut''', more commonly known as the '''Juggernaut''', the '''Republic Juggernaut''', the '''clone turbo tank''', or simply the '''turbo tank''', was a heavily armed and [[armor]]ed ten-wheeled [[tank]] manufactured by [[Kuat Drive Yards]]. They were used as military [[transport]]s by the [[Grand Army of the Republic]] during the [[Clone Wars]]. Following the [[Galactic Republic]]'s [[Proclamation of the New Order|transition]] into the [[Galactic Empire]], the A6 was used by the Empire. The HAVw A6 Juggernaut was the successor of the [[Juggernaut A5]] and itself succeeded by the [[HCVw A9 turbo tank]].

==Characteristics==
{{Quote|Absolutely gigantic-carrying up to three hundred troopers-and armed with a turret-mounted heavy laser cannon, a rapid repeating laser cannon, four antipersonnel cannons, and two concussion missile launchers.|Pitt Onoran, on the HAVw A6|<ref name="RebelFiles">''[[Star Wars: The Rebel Files]]''</ref>}}
[[File:JuggernautROTS.jpg|thumb|left|250px|The Juggernaut was an armored powerhouse on the battlefield.]]
A massive, ten-wheeled heavy assault vehicle manufactured by [[Kuat Drive Yards]] for use by the [[Galactic Republic]]'s [[Grand Army of the Republic|clone army]], the HAVw A6 Juggernaut was used as both a [[troop carrier]] and a main battle [[tank]]. The Juggernaut was twice the size with 5 times the weight of the Republic's [[All Terrain Tactical Enforcer]] and was one of the largest ground vehicles built by KDY,<ref name="Collapse" /> measuring to 49.4 [[meter]]s in length.<ref name="Starfighters" /> It had a boxy [[hull]] covered in sloping thermal superconducting [[armor]] that could disperse both the heat and energy of incoming fire over a broad area rather than ablating or reflecting damage. A powerful, high-output [[reactor]] powered the tank's complex all-wheel drive system that could deliver power to each wheel as needed<ref name="Collapse" /> for a maximum speed of 160 [[Kilometer per hour|kilometers per hour]].<ref name="Starfighters" /> Each wheel was independently mounted and suspended which added a layer of complexity to an already complicated drive system, but allowed for a surprising amount of articulation.<ref name="Collapse" />

The Juggernaut's interior was surprisingly spacious and could carry up to a [[company]] of 300 fully equipped [[clone trooper]]s in individual jump seats on two decks. The forward section of the hull contained the vehicle's command deck with positions for the [[pilot]], co-pilot, [[vehicle commander]], [[navigator]], [[engineer]], comms operators, and two forward [[gunner]]s. Directly aft of the command deck was a small trauma bay staffed by a [[medic]] or [[medical droid]] for treatment to wounded troopers. An extendable observation pod could be raised on a mast over the command deck to give a spotter an unparalleled view of the battlefield. A small bunker mounted aft of the troop compartment housed a backup command deck and the rest of the gunnery stations.<ref name="Collapse" />

Juggernauts also brought the destructive potential of an entire armored [[regiment]] to the battlefield.<ref name="Collapse" /> A6 models were armed with a broad array of two anti-personnel [[laser cannon]]s,<ref name="Ultimate Star Wars" /> a dorsal [[turret]]-mounted rapid-repeating rotary [[laser]] [[cannon]]<ref name="Collapse" /> manned by a [[clone gunner]],<ref name="Holocron Heist">{{TCW|Holocron Heist}}</ref> two retractable hull-mounted anti-personnel flex ordnance [[launcher]]s, two twin forward-mounted anti-personnel light [[blaster cannon]]s, and one dorsal turret-mounted [[heavy laser cannon]]. The armament granted the vehicle commander a bit of tactical flexibility.<ref name="Collapse" /> According to Kuat Drive Yards, the A6 could deliver an attack to a small area that on an unshielded target would inflict heat damage equivalent alone to a small tactical nuclear device.<ref name="Republic and Separatist Ships (2)">{{EncyclopediaCite|Republic and Separatist Ships (2)}}</ref>

==History==
===Aid mission to Langston===
[[File:HAVwA6Juggernaut-FFp41.png|thumb|right|300px|The HAVw A6 Juggernaut (example pictured) was used by the Galactic Republic during the Clone Wars.]]
HAVw A6 Juggernauts saw use by the [[Galactic Republic]] during the [[Clone Wars]] against the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems]].<ref name="Holocron Heist" /> In [[22 BBY]],<ref name="Brotherhood Date">The events of [[Brotherhood (novel)|''Brotherhood'']], and therefore the [[aid mission to Langston]], occur in [[22 BBY]] according to ''[[Star Wars: Timelines]]''</ref> after the resource-rich world of [[Langston]] suffered a [[Attack on Langston|Confederate attack]] during the early [[Standard week|weeks]] of the war, Republic forces operating under the [[302nd Battalion]] were deployed to Langston for an [[Aid mission to Langston|aid mission]], during which a Juggernaut was filled with medical supplies and dispatched to [[Langston's largest continent]] as part of a [[convoy]]. Filled up with more medical supplies than [[trooper]]s, the Juggernaut made its away to the continent, where [[Jedi General]] [[Anakin Skywalker]] ordered a [[Cloning|clone]] trooper to bring the first ten supply crates to a neighborhood in [[Sector N-seven-A-I]].<ref name="Brotherhood">[[Brotherhood (novel)|''Brotherhood'']]</ref>

Further ordered by the [[general]] to meet up with Captain [[Raptor]] in [[Fong-Tim Square]] to split up the rest of the crates once that was done, the trooper then made their way back to the Juggernaut to complete Skywalker's orders. Familiar with the damage sand could do to [[wheel]]ed vehicles due to his past on the [[desert]] planet [[Tatooine]], Skywalker quickly added to watch out for the world's sand lest it get into the vehicle's gears. Once the Juggernaut and the rest of the convoy began its journey, Skywalker took the moment to try and get sand out of his [[Cybernetics|cybernetic]] [[Limb|hand]].<ref name="Brotherhood" />

===First Battle of Felucia===
[[File:Rescue_on_Felucia.jpg|thumb|left|300px|A juggernaut on Felucia used to combat the Separatists.]]
One HAVw A6 Juggernaut was deployed at the [[First Battle of Felucia]]. During the battle, [[Jedi Commander]] [[Ahsoka Tano]]'s Juggernaut was accompanied by two [[All Terrain Tactical Enforcer|AT-TEs]] on a [[jungle]] patrol. Under the leadership of [[Padawan]] Tano, the tanks pushed forward against a large Separatist [[Separatist Droid Army|force]] and she believed that the [[droid]]s were in full retreat, when in actuality they were regrouping to overwhelm Republic forces. [[Low Altitude Assault Transport/infantry|LAAT/i gunships]] arrived from the [[104th Battalion]] to evacuate Republic forces on Felucia and after evacuating [[Jedi]] Generals Anakin Skywalker and [[Obi-Wan Kenobi]] and their forces, they headed to Tano's position.<ref name="Holocron Heist" />

Tano refused to retreat, believing that they were winning over the Separatist forces. Skywalker ordered that the [[gunship]]s land in front of the Juggernaut and Republic forces in order to evacuate the [[clone trooper]]s and convince Tano to retreat from the engagement. After Tano and her [[trooper]]s evacuated and were airborne, Tano looked on to see her Juggernaut explode in a fiery destruction by the combined forces of Separatist droids and vehicles which also knocked out other, nearby Republic vehicles.<ref name="Holocron Heist" />

===Continued deployment===
{{Quote|A heavy-assault vehicle left over from the Clone Wars and every bit as dangerous as an AT-AT. ''[&hellip]'' The Juggernaut also has a little brother, the scaled-down HCVwA9 turbo tank.|Pitt Onoran, in a letter to Mon Mothma|<ref name="RebelFiles" />}}
[[File:RCMO-ToC-CotR.png|thumb|right|250px|Several Juggernauts patrol outside the Republic Center for Military Operations on Coruscant]]
The HAVw A6 Juggernaut again saw action on [[Felucia]] during another [[Second Battle of Felucia|battle]] against Separatist forces. During the attack, at least two Juggernauts accompanied AT-TE [[walker]]s as they [[Artillery|bombarded]] a [[Separatist outpost (Felucia)|Separatist outpost]].<ref name="Padawan Lost">{{TCW|Padawan Lost}}</ref> Juggernauts were also used on the Republic capital of [[Coruscant]] and could be seen deployed at the [[Imperial Inspectorate HQ|Republic Center for Military Operations]].<ref name="CotR">''[[Collapse of the Republic]]''</ref> Several HAVw A6 Juggernauts were utilized extensively in the [[Battle of Kashyyyk]] alongside [[All Terrain Attack Pod|AT-APs]], [[All Terrain Reconnaissance Transport|AT-RTs]], and [[Infantry Support Platform]]s, where they faced against Separatist [[NR-N99 Persuader-class droid enforcer|NR-N99 tank droids]] and [[DSD1 dwarf spider droid]]s.<ref name="ROTS" />

By the end of the war,<ref name="Devil's Deal trivia gallery">{{SW|url=devil-s-deal-trivia-gallery|text=Tech's Notes: "Devil's Deal" Trivia Gallery}} {{C|Slide 6}} establishes that the [[HCVw A9 turbo tank]] saw use in the [[Clone Wars]], so it must have been introduced by the end of the conflict.</ref> a successor model to the HAvw A6, the [[HCVw A9 turbo tank]],<ref name="UVG">''[[Star Wars: Rogue One: The Ultimate Visual Guide]]''</ref> had been introduced and saw use in the conflict<ref name="Devil's Deal trivia gallery" /> despite the continued deployment of the HAVw A6.<ref name="ROTS" /> Due to its success and effectiveness, the HAVw A6 continued to be utilized by the [[Galactic Empire]], [[Proclamation of the New Order|successor]] to the Galactic Republic.<ref name="Starfighters" /> The regime also made use of the HCVw A9.<ref name="Rogue One">''[[Rogue One: A Star Wars Story]]''</ref> Rebel General [[Pitt Onoran]] included the HAVw A6 in his breakdown of Imperial armored vehicles and delivered it as a letter to [[Mon Mothma]], [[Commander-in-Chief]] of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic]].<ref name="RebelFiles" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:JuggernautHothConcept-MOTESB.png|thumb|left|180px|[[Battle of Hoth]] concept art by [[Joe Johnston]] featuring the wheeled tank that would be the basis of the HAVw A6.]]
The HAVw A6 Juggernaut first appeared in [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Star Wars'': Episode III: ''Revenge of the Sith'']] released in [[2005]].<ref name="ROTS" /> The Juggernaut originated from a concept of a ten-wheeled tank used by the Empire during the [[Battle of Hoth]] by [[Joe Johnston]] for [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''Star Wars'': Episode V: ''The Empire Strikes Back'']] before the [[All Terrain Armored Transport|AT-AT walkers]] were chosen over tanks. [[George Lucas]] later revisited this design when seeking to bridge the designs of the original and prequel trilogies. Unlike the original concept, the HAVw A6 was made larger and was better armored.<ref name="DB-clone turbo tank">{{DB|vehicle|turbotank|clone turbo tank}}</ref>
